local ScriptShield = {
    GatewayUrl = "https://your-domain.com/api/v1/script-auth",
    GatewayUrls = {},
    ScriptId = "ENTER_YOUR_SCRIPT_ID_HERE",
    ApiSecret = "",
    EncryptionKey = "aes_encryption_key_shared",
    Version = "1.0.0",
    MaxRetries = 3,
    RetryDelay = 2,
    EnableRuntimeHardening = true,
    EnableAntiTamper = true,
    EnableAntiDebug = true,
    EnableAntiHook = true,
    EnableAntiDump = true,
    EnableAntiEnv = true,
    EnableAntiEmulation = true,
    EnableAntiPatch = true,
    EnableAntiVm = true,
    EnableAntiSpoof = true,
    EnableAntiReplay = true,
    EnableAntiKeySharing = true,
    EnableAntiHwidSpoof = true,
    EnableAntiBypass = true,
    RuntimeCheckInterval = 15,
    AllowedExecutors = {},
    -- Key system configuration
    Keyless = false,              -- If true, no key required — loads immediately
    GetKeyLink = "",             -- Linkvertise/LootLabs/custom URL to get a key
    KeyStorageName = "FireLabKey", -- Where to store the key (setclipboard or file)
    KeyPromptTitle = "FireLab Security",
    KeyPromptDescription = "Enter your license key to continue",
    KeyPromptButton = "Get Key",
    KeyPromptPlaceholder = "LUA-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX",
    KeyPromptTimeout = 120,      -- Seconds before player is kicked for not entering a key
}

local function waitSeconds(seconds)
    if task and task.wait then
        task.wait(seconds)
    else
        wait(seconds)
    end
end

local function kickPlayer(message)
    local gameTable = game
    if type(gameTable) == "table" and type(gameTable.Kick) == "function" then
        pcall(gameTable.Kick, gameTable, "[FireLab Security] " .. tostring(message))
    end
end

local function failSecurity(message)
    warn("[FireLab Security] " .. tostring(message))
    kickPlayer(message)
    error("[FireLab Security] " .. tostring(message))
end

local function getGatewayUrls()
    local urls = {}
    if ScriptShield.GatewayUrls and #ScriptShield.GatewayUrls > 0 then
        for _, url in ipairs(ScriptShield.GatewayUrls) do
            if url and url ~= "" then urls[#urls + 1] = url end
        end
    elseif ScriptShield.GatewayUrl and ScriptShield.GatewayUrl ~= "" then
        urls[1] = ScriptShield.GatewayUrl
    end
    return urls
end

local function getExecutorName()
    if getexecutorname then
        local ok, name = pcall(getexecutorname)
        if ok and name then return tostring(name) end
    end
    if identifyexecutor then
        local ok, name = pcall(identifyexecutor)
        if ok and name then return tostring(name) end
    end
    if syn and syn.executor_name then
        local ok, name = pcall(syn.executor_name)
        if ok and name then return tostring(name) end
    end
    return "UnknownExecutor"
end

local function collectMachineHWID()
    local values = {}

    local function add(value)
        if value and tostring(value) ~= "" then
            values[#values + 1] = tostring(value)
        end
    end

    local function call(name)
        local fn = rawget(_G, name)
        if type(fn) == "function" then
            local ok, value = pcall(fn)
            add(value)
        end
    end

    local function callTable(tableValue, name)
        if type(tableValue) == "table" and type(tableValue[name]) == "function" then
            local ok, value = pcall(tableValue[name])
            add(value)
        end
    end

    call("gethwid")
    callTable(rawget(_G, "syn"), "get_hwid")
    callTable(rawget(_G, "fluxus"), "get_hwid")
    callTable(rawget(_G, "evon"), "get_hwid")
    callTable(rawget(_G, "krnl"), "get_hwid")

    table.sort(values)

    if #values == 0 then
        return getExecutorName() .. ":" .. tostring(os.clock()), values
    end

    return values[1], values
end

local function isSpoofedHWID(value)
    value = tostring(value or "")
    local lowerValue = value:lower()

    if #value < 8 then return true end
    if value:match("^0+$") then return true end
    if value:match("^(%w)%1+$") then return true end
    if lowerValue:find("spoof", 1, true) then return true end
    if lowerValue:find("test", 1, true) then return true end
    if lowerValue:find("null", 1, true) then return true end
    if lowerValue:find("none", 1, true) then return true end

    return false
end

local function getMachineHWID()
    local hwid, values = collectMachineHWID()

    if ScriptShield.EnableAntiHwidSpoof or ScriptShield.EnableAntiSpoof then
        if isSpoofedHWID(hwid) then
            failSecurity("Spoofed hardware id detected.")
        end

        local first = values[1]
        for _, value in ipairs(values) do
            if first and value ~= first then
                failSecurity("Hardware id provider mismatch detected.")
            end
        end
    end

    return hwid
end

local function jsonEscape(value)
    value = tostring(value)
    value = value:gsub("\\", "\\\\")
    value = value:gsub('"', '\\"')
    value = value:gsub("\b", "\\b")
    value = value:gsub("\f", "\\f")
    value = value:gsub("\n", "\\n")
    value = value:gsub("\r", "\\r")
    value = value:gsub("\t", "\\t")
    return value
end

local function canonicalStringify(value)
    local valueType = type(value)

    if valueType == "string" then
        return '"' .. jsonEscape(value) .. '"'
    end

    if valueType == "number" or valueType == "boolean" then
        return tostring(value)
    end

    if valueType == "table" then
        local isArray = true
        for key in pairs(value) do
            if type(key) ~= "number" then
                isArray = false
                break
            end
        end

        if isArray then
            local parts = {}
            for i = 1, #value do
                table.insert(parts, canonicalStringify(value[i]))
            end
            return "[" .. table.concat(parts, ",") .. "]"
        end

        local keys = {}
        for key in pairs(value) do
            table.insert(keys, key)
        end
        table.sort(keys)

        local parts = {}
        for _, key in ipairs(keys) do
            table.insert(parts, '"' .. jsonEscape(key) .. '":' .. canonicalStringify(value[key]))
        end
        return "{" .. table.concat(parts, ",") .. "}"
    end

    return "null"
end

local function parseJSON(str)
    str = tostring(str or "")
    str = str:gsub("\\/", "/")
    str = str:gsub('\\"', '"')
    str = str:gsub("\\n", "\n")
    str = str:gsub("\\r", "\r")
    str = str:gsub("\\t", "\t")

    local obj = {}
    for key, value in str:gmatch('"([^"]+)"%s*:%s*"([^"]*)"') do
        obj[key] = value
    end
    for key, value in str:gmatch('"([^"]+)"%s*:%s*(%d+%.?%d*)') do
        obj[key] = tonumber(value)
    end
    for key in str:gmatch('"([^"]+)"%s*:%s*(true)') do
        obj[key] = true
    end
    for key in str:gmatch('"([^"]+)"%s*:%s*(false)') do
        obj[key] = false
    end
    return obj
end

local function split(value, separator)
    local parts = {}
    local pattern = "([^" .. separator .. "]+)"
    for part in tostring(value):gmatch(pattern) do
        table.insert(parts, part)
    end
    return parts
end

local function getResponseBody(response)
    if not response then return nil end
    return response.Body or response.body or response.Content or response.content
end

local function getResponseStatusCode(response)
    if not response then return nil end
    return response.StatusCode or response.statusCode or response.Status or response.status
end

local function generateHMACSignature(body, secret)
    if not secret or secret == "" then
        return nil
    end

    if crypt and crypt.hmac then
        return crypt.hmac("sha256", secret, body)
    end

    if http and http.hmac then
        return http.hmac("sha256", secret, body)
    end

    error("[FireLab] Executor does not expose an HMAC helper, but API_SECRET_KEY is enabled.")
end

local function requestGateway(url, payload, headers)
    local requestFunctions = {}
    if syn and syn.request then table.insert(requestFunctions, syn.request) end
    if request then table.insert(requestFunctions, request) end
    if http_request then table.insert(requestFunctions, http_request) end
    if http and http.request then table.insert(requestFunctions, http.request) end

    if #requestFunctions == 0 then
        error("[FireLab] Critical failure: Executor is missing HTTP request capability.")
    end

    local lastError = nil
    for _, requestFunc in ipairs(requestFunctions) do
        local requestBodies = {
            { Url = url, Method = "POST", Headers = headers, Body = payload },
            { url = url, method = "POST", headers = headers, body = payload },
            { URL = url, Method = "POST", Headers = headers, Body = payload }
        }

        for _, body in ipairs(requestBodies) do
            local ok, response = pcall(requestFunc, body)
            if ok then
                local status = getResponseStatusCode(response)
                local responseBody = getResponseBody(response)
                if status == 200 and responseBody then
                    return responseBody
                end

                if status == 401 or status == 403 then
                    local errBody = getResponseBody(response)
                    local errData = parseJSON(errBody or "")
                    local errMsg = errData.error or "Authentication rejected"
                    kickPlayer(errMsg)
                    error("[FireLab] " .. errMsg)
                end

                lastError = "HTTP " .. tostring(status or "No Status")
            else
                lastError = tostring(response)
            end
        end
    end

    error(lastError or "No response")
end

local function sendRequest(payload, headers, retryCount, gatewayIndex)
    retryCount = retryCount or 0
    gatewayIndex = gatewayIndex or 1

    local urls = getGatewayUrls()
    local url = urls[gatewayIndex]
    if not url then
        error("[FireLab] Fatal error: Secure gateway nodes are currently unreachable.")
    end

    local ok, result = pcall(requestGateway, url, payload, headers)
    if ok then
        return result
    end

    warn("[FireLab] Gateway handshake failed (" .. tostring(result) .. ")")

    if retryCount < ScriptShield.MaxRetries then
        local waitTime = ScriptShield.RetryDelay * (2 ^ retryCount)
        waitSeconds(waitTime)
        return sendRequest(payload, headers, retryCount + 1, gatewayIndex)
    end

    if gatewayIndex < #urls then
        return sendRequest(payload, headers, 0, gatewayIndex + 1)
    end

    error("[FireLab] Fatal error: Secure gateway nodes are currently unreachable.")
end

local function decryptPayload(encryptedModule, key)
    if not encryptedModule or encryptedModule == "" then
        error("[FireLab] Empty encrypted execution payload.")
    end

    if not crypt or not crypt.aes then
        error("[FireLab] Executor does not expose crypt.aes for AES-GCM decryption.")
    end

    local parts = split(encryptedModule, ".")
    local iv, ciphertext, tag = parts[1], parts[2], parts[3]

    if not iv or not ciphertext or not tag then
        error("[FireLab] Corrupt encrypted execution payload format.")
    end

    local success, decrypted = pcall(function()
        return crypt.aes.decrypt(ciphertext, key, iv, "GCM", tag)
    end)

    if success and decrypted and decrypted ~= "" then
        return decrypted
    end

    success, decrypted = pcall(function()
        return crypt.aes.decrypt(ciphertext, key, iv, tag, "GCM")
    end)

    if success and decrypted and decrypted ~= "" then
        return decrypted
    end

    error("[FireLab] Decryption failed. Check ENCRYPTION_KEY and executor AES-GCM support.")
end

local function getFunctionEnv(fn)
    local getfenvFn = rawget(_G, "getfenv")
    if type(getfenvFn) == "function" then
        local ok, env = pcall(getfenvFn, fn)
        if ok and type(env) == "table" then
            return env
        end
    end
    return nil
end

local function isDumperEnvironment(env)
    return type(env) == "table" and (env._25ms or env.ce_like_loadstring_fn or env.luau or env.process or env.fs or env.net or env.roblox or env.fakegame)
end

local function checkEnvLoggerSurface()
    local getfenvFn = rawget(_G, "getfenv")
    if type(getfenvFn) ~= "function" then
        return true
    end

    local ok, env = pcall(getfenvFn, 1)
    if not ok or type(env) ~= "table" then
        return true
    end

    if type(env.getgenv) == "function" then
        local genvOk, genv = pcall(env.getgenv, env)
        if genvOk and type(genv) == "table" and rawequal(genv, env) and env.loadstring == env.load then
            return false, "env logger fake getfenv/getgenv"
        end
    end

    if type(env.require) == "function" then
        local requireOk, requireResult = pcall(env.require, "__ScriptShield_nonexistent_probe__")
        if requireOk and type(requireResult) == "table" then
            return false, "env logger fake require"
        end
    end

    return true
end

local function loaderFunction(source)
    local loadFn = rawget(_G, "load")
    if type(loadFn) == "function" then
        local ok, execute = pcall(loadFn, source, "ScriptShieldPayload")
        if ok and type(execute) == "function" and not isDumperEnvironment(getFunctionEnv(execute)) then
            return execute
        end

        ok, execute = pcall(loadFn, function() return source end, "ScriptShieldPayload")
        if ok and type(execute) == "function" and not isDumperEnvironment(getFunctionEnv(execute)) then
            return execute
        end
    end

    if loadstring then
        local ok, execute = pcall(loadstring, source, "ScriptShieldPayload")
        if ok and type(execute) == "function" and not isDumperEnvironment(getFunctionEnv(execute)) then
            return execute
        end
    end

    return nil, "loadstring/load unavailable or blocked by dumper environment"
end

local function checkAllowedExecutor()
    if not ScriptShield.EnableAntiEnv or #ScriptShield.AllowedExecutors == 0 then
        return true
    end

    local executor = getExecutorName():lower()
    for _, allowed in ipairs(ScriptShield.AllowedExecutors) do
        local normalized = tostring(allowed):lower()
        if executor == normalized or executor:find(normalized, 1, true) then
            return true
        end
    end

    return false, "unexpected executor: " .. getExecutorName()
end

local function checkDebugHardening()
    if not ScriptShield.EnableAntiDebug then
        return true
    end

    local debugLibrary = rawget(_G, "debug")
    if not debugLibrary then
        return true
    end

    if type(debugLibrary) ~= "table" then
        return false, "debug library tampered"
    end

    if type(debugLibrary.gethook) ~= "function" or type(debugLibrary.getinfo) ~= "function" or type(debugLibrary.getlocal) ~= "function" then
        return false, "debug library tampered"
    end

    local hookOk, hook = pcall(debugLibrary.gethook)
    if hookOk and hook then
        return false, "debug hook detected"
    end

    return true
end

local function checkFunctionIntegrity()
    if not ScriptShield.EnableAntiHook and not ScriptShield.EnableAntiPatch then
        return true
    end

    local functionNames = { "loadstring", "load", "setmetatable", "rawset", "rawget" }
    for _, name in ipairs(functionNames) do
        local value = rawget(_G, name)
        if value ~= nil and type(value) ~= "function" then
            return false, name .. " tampered"
        end
    end

    local debugLibrary = rawget(_G, "debug")
    if debugLibrary ~= nil then
        if type(debugLibrary) ~= "table" then
            return false, "debug library tampered"
        end
        for _, name in ipairs({ "gethook", "sethook", "getinfo", "getlocal", "getupvalue" }) do
            local value = rawget(debugLibrary, name)
            if value ~= nil and type(value) ~= "function" then
                return false, "debug." .. name .. " tampered"
            end
        end
    end

    return true
end

local function checkSuspiciousGlobals()
    local names = {}

    if ScriptShield.EnableAntiEmulation or ScriptShield.EnableAntiBypass then
        for _, name in ipairs({ "syn", "gethui", "getscriptbytecode", "getconstants", "getupvalues", "getupvalue", "getgc", "filtergc", "filter_gc", "getgc_objects", "getinstances", "getnilinstances", "getconnections", "getrawmetatable", "setrawmetatable", "hookfunction", "hookfunc", "hookmetamethod", "hooksignal", "replaceclosure", "replacefunction", "replacefunc", "detour_function", "detourfunction", "firecallback", "firetouchinterest", "queue_on_teleport", "setclipboard", "setfflag", "gethiddenproperty", "sethiddenproperty", "getregistry", "getreg", "getproto", "getprotos", "getscriptclosure", "getscriptfunction", "getscriptcaller", "getcallingscript", "getmodules", "get_scripts", "getscripts", "getloadedmodules", "getrunningcripts", "getmenv", "gettenv", "getrenv", "getrenvglobal", "getrenvshared", "getcontext", "getidentity", "checkcaller", "checkclosure", "protectfunction", "protectclosure", "protectinstance", "restorefunction", "restorefunc", "restoreclosure", "clonefunc", "clonefunction", "setreadonly", "setconstant", "setupvalue", "getconstant", "saveinstance", "decompile", "dumpstring", "getscripthash", "getfunctionhash", "getsenv", "isfunctionhooked", "issignalhooked", "isconnectionenabled", "is_executor_closure", "isourclosure", "isourcallstack", "fluxus", "krnl", "evon", "solara", "scriptware", "electron", "warden", "sentinel", "kali", "delta", "venus", "hydrogen", "dumb", "_25ms", "_25msEQ", "_25msOR", "_25msAND", "_25msINDEX", "ce_like_loadstring_fn", "luau", "process", "fs", "net", "roblox", "fakegame", "__LARRY_NORMALIZE_SOURCE", "__LARRY_PATCH_ENV", "__PENGUE_ENV_LOGGER", "ENV_LOGGER" }) do
            table.insert(names, name)
        end
    end

    if ScriptShield.EnableAntiBypass then
        for _, name in ipairs({ "gethiddenproperty", "sethiddenproperty" }) do
            table.insert(names, name)
        end
    end

    for _, name in ipairs(names) do
        if rawget(_G, name) ~= nil then
            return false, "bypass surface detected: " .. name
        end
    end

    return true
end

local function checkVirtualEnvironment()
    if not ScriptShield.EnableAntiVm then
        return true
    end

    local executor = getExecutorName():lower()
    for _, pattern in ipairs({ "virtualbox", "vmware", "qemu", "virtual", "vagrant", "parallel", "sandbox", "wine", "container", "hyper-v" }) do
        if executor:find(pattern, 1, true) then
            return false, "virtualized execution detected"
        end
    end

    return true
end

local function checkRealRobloxEnvironment()
    if not ScriptShield.EnableAntiEnv then
        return true
    end

    local gameOk, gameTable = pcall(function() return game end)
    if not gameOk or type(gameTable) ~= "table" then
        return false, "Roblox game missing"
    end

    local runOk, runService = pcall(gameTable.GetService, gameTable, "RunService")
    if not runOk or type(runService) ~= "table" then
        return false, "RunService missing"
    end

    if type(runService.IsStudio) ~= "function" then
        return false, "RunService tampered"
    end

    local studioOk, isStudio = pcall(runService.IsStudio, runService)
    if not studioOk then
        return false, "RunService inspection failed"
    end

    if isStudio then
        return false, "Studio execution detected"
    end

    return true
end

local function checkLoaderIntegrity()
    if not ScriptShield.EnableAntiTamper and not ScriptShield.EnableAntiPatch then
        return true
    end

    local ok, loader = loaderFunction("return true")
    if not ok or type(loader) ~= "function" then
        return false, "loader patched"
    end

    local resultOk, result = pcall(loader)
    if not resultOk or result ~= true then
        return false, "loader integrity failed"
    end

    return true
end

local function generateReplayNonce()
    local tickValue = 0
    if tick then
        local ok, value = pcall(tick)
        if ok then tickValue = value end
    end

    return tostring(os.time()) .. ":" .. tostring(tickValue) .. ":" .. tostring(math.random(1, 2147483647))
end

local function checkReplayGuard()
    if not ScriptShield.EnableAntiReplay then
        return ""
    end

    local nonce = generateReplayNonce()
    local replayStore = rawget(_G, "__ScriptShieldReplayStore") or {}

    if replayStore[nonce] then
        error("[FireLab] Replay nonce reused.")
    end

    replayStore[nonce] = os.clock()

    local count = 0
    for key in pairs(replayStore) do
        count = count + 1
        if count > 256 then
            replayStore[key] = nil
        end
    end

    rawset(_G, "__ScriptShieldReplayStore", replayStore)
    return nonce
end

local function checkKeySharingGuard(hwid)
    if not ScriptShield.EnableAntiKeySharing then
        return true
    end

    local keyScope = rawget(_G, "__ScriptShieldKeyScope") or {}
    if keyScope[ScriptShield.ScriptId] and keyScope[ScriptShield.ScriptId] ~= hwid then
        failSecurity("key sharing detected")
    end

    keyScope[ScriptShield.ScriptId] = hwid
    rawset(_G, "__ScriptShieldKeyScope", keyScope)
    return true
end

local function hardenRuntime()
    if not ScriptShield.EnableRuntimeHardening then
        return true
    end

    local checks = {
        checkAllowedExecutor(),
        checkDebugHardening(),
        checkFunctionIntegrity(),
        checkSuspiciousGlobals(),
        checkEnvLoggerSurface(),
        checkVirtualEnvironment(),
        checkRealRobloxEnvironment(),
        checkLoaderIntegrity()
    }

    for _, check in ipairs(checks) do
        local ok, message = check[1], check[2]
        if not ok then
            failSecurity("Runtime hardening failed: " .. tostring(message))
        end
    end

    return true
end

--==================================================
-- Key System UI (prompt, store, retrieve)
--==================================================

local function createKeyPrompt(onSubmit, onClose)
    local players = game:GetService("Players")
    local player = players.LocalPlayer
    if not player then return nil end

    local gui = Instance.new("ScreenGui")

    end)

    input:CaptureFocus()
    return gui
end

local function saveKey(key)
    pcall(function()
        if writefile then
            writefile(ScriptShield.KeyStorageName .. ".txt", key)
        end
    end)
    pcall(function()
        if setclipboard then
            setclipboard(key)
        end
    end)
end

local function loadSavedKey()
    local key = nil
    pcall(function()
        if readfile and isfile then
            local fname = ScriptShield.KeyStorageName .. ".txt"
            if isfile(fname) then
                key = readfile(fname)
                key = key:match("^%s*(.-)%s*$")
            end
        end
    end)
    return key
end

local function clearSavedKey()
    pcall(function()
        if delfile then
            delfile(ScriptShield.KeyStorageName .. ".txt")
        end
    end)
end

--==================================================
-- Main Load function
--==================================================

    local hardeningOk, hardeningError = pcall(hardenRuntime)
    if not hardeningOk then
        return false, hardeningError
    end

    local hwidOk, hwidOrError = pcall(getMachineHWID)
    if not hwidOk then
        return false, hwidOrError
    end

    local hwid = hwidOrError
    local keySharingOk, keySharingError = pcall(checkKeySharingGuard, hwid)
    if not keySharingOk then
        return false, keySharingError
    end

    local nonce = checkReplayGuard()
    local rawPayload = canonicalStringify({
        key = licenseKey,
        hwid = hwid,
        scriptId = ScriptShield.ScriptId,
        nonce = nonce,
        timestamp = os.time(),
        executor = getExecutorName()
    })

    local headers = {
        ["Content-Type"] = "application/json",
        ["User-Agent"] = "ScriptShieldRuntimeLoader/" .. ScriptShield.Version
    }

    local signature = generateHMACSignature(rawPayload, ScriptShield.ApiSecret)
    if signature then
        headers["X-Signature"] = signature
    end

    local responseBody = sendRequest(rawPayload, headers)
    local data = parseJSON(responseBody)

    if data.error then
        kickPlayer(tostring(data.error))
        error("[FireLab Auth Failure] " .. tostring(data.error))
    end

    if data.timestamp then
        local serverAge = math.abs((os.time() * 1000) - tonumber(data.timestamp))
        if serverAge and serverAge > 300000 then
            error("[FireLab] Stale authentication response.")
        end
    end

    local encryptedModule = data.encrypted_module or data.iv .. "." .. data.ciphertext .. "." .. data.tag
    local decryptedCode = decryptPayload(encryptedModule, ScriptShield.EncryptionKey)

    if not decryptedCode or decryptedCode == "" then
        error("[FireLab] Decryption failed. Corrupt session packet.")
    end

    local execute, compileError = loaderFunction(decryptedCode)
    if not execute then
        error("[FireLab] Payload compilation error: " .. tostring(compileError))
    end

    return true, execute
end

function ScriptShield.Authenticate(licenseKey)
    -- Keyless mode: skip key auth entirely, use HWID only
    if ScriptShield.Keyless then
        licenseKey = "KEYLESS_" .. tostring(os.time())
        local ok, execute = ScriptShield.Load(licenseKey)
        if ok and execute then
            execute()
        end
        return ok, execute
    end

    -- If no key provided, try to load saved key or show prompt
    if not licenseKey or licenseKey == "" then
        licenseKey = loadSavedKey()
    end

    if not licenseKey or licenseKey == "" then
        -- Show key prompt UI and wait for user input
        local keyResult = nil
        local promptClosed = false

        createKeyPrompt(function(key, errorLabel, gui)
            -- Validate the key by attempting to load
            local ok, err = pcall(function()
                local loadOk, loadErr = ScriptShield.Load(key)
                if loadOk then
                    saveKey(key)
                    keyResult = key
                    gui:Destroy()
                else
                    errorLabel.Text = tostring(loadErr):gsub("%[FireLab%]%s*", "")
                end
            end)
            if not ok then
                errorLabel.Text = tostring(err):gsub("%[FireLab%]%s*", "")
            end
        end, function()
            promptClosed = true
        end)

        -- Wait for the prompt to resolve (with timeout)
        local waited = 0
        while not keyResult and not promptClosed do
            waitSeconds(0.5)
            waited = waited + 0.5
            if waited >= ScriptShield.KeyPromptTimeout then
                kickPlayer("Key entry timed out. You have " .. ScriptShield.KeyPromptTimeout .. " seconds to enter a key.")
                return false, "Key entry timed out"
            end
        end

        if keyResult then
            local ok, execute = ScriptShield.Load(keyResult)
            if ok and execute then
                execute()
            end
            return ok, execute
        else
            kickPlayer("Authentication cancelled")
            return false, "Authentication cancelled"
        end
    end

    -- Key provided directly
    local ok, execute = ScriptShield.Load(licenseKey)
    if ok then
        saveKey(licenseKey)
        execute()
    else
        -- Key failed, clear saved key and show prompt
        clearSavedKey()

        local keyResult = nil
        local promptClosed = false

        createKeyPrompt(function(key, errorLabel, gui)
            local checkOk, err = pcall(function()
                local loadOk, loadErr = ScriptShield.Load(key)
                if loadOk then
                    saveKey(key)
                    keyResult = key
                    gui:Destroy()
                else
                    errorLabel.Text = tostring(loadErr):gsub("%[FireLab%]%s*", "")
                end
            end)
            if not checkOk then
                errorLabel.Text = tostring(err):gsub("%[FireLab%]%s*", "")
            end
        end, function()
            promptClosed = true
        end)

        local waited2 = 0
        while not keyResult and not promptClosed do
            waitSeconds(0.5)
            waited2 = waited2 + 0.5
            if waited2 >= ScriptShield.KeyPromptTimeout then
                kickPlayer("Key entry timed out. You have " .. ScriptShield.KeyPromptTimeout .. " seconds to enter a key.")
                return false, "Key entry timed out"
            end
        end

        if keyResult then
            local ok2, execute2 = ScriptShield.Load(keyResult)
            if ok2 and execute2 then
                execute2()
            end
            return ok2, execute2
        else
            kickPlayer("Authentication cancelled")
            return false, "Authentication cancelled"
        end
    end

    -- Runtime key validation loop
    if ScriptShield.EnableRuntimeHardening and ScriptShield.RuntimeCheckInterval > 0 then
        task.spawn(function()
            while true do
                task.wait(ScriptShield.RuntimeCheckInterval)
                local checkOk, checkErr = pcall(function()
                    local hwid = getMachineHWID()
                    local nonce = checkReplayGuard()
                    local rawPayload = canonicalStringify({
                        key = licenseKey,
                        hwid = hwid,
                        scriptId = ScriptShield.ScriptId,
                        nonce = nonce,
                        timestamp = os.time(),
                        executor = getExecutorName()
                    })
                    local headers = {
                        ["Content-Type"] = "application/json",
                        ["User-Agent"] = "ScriptShieldRuntimeChecker/" .. ScriptShield.Version
                    }
                    local signature = generateHMACSignature(rawPayload, ScriptShield.ApiSecret)
                    if signature then
                        headers["X-Signature"] = signature
                    end
                    local responseBody = sendRequest(rawPayload, headers)
                    local data = parseJSON(responseBody)
                    if data.error then
                        kickPlayer(tostring(data.error))
                        clearSavedKey()
                        error("[FireLab Runtime] Key invalidated: " .. tostring(data.error))
                    end
                end)
                if not checkOk then
                    kickPlayer("Runtime security check failed")
                    break
                end
            end
        end)
    end

    return ok, execute
end

return ScriptShield

## Summary

I located and read all four requested files in the project at `C:\Users\ziyad\Projects\script-shield-platform`. All files were read in full. Below are the findings for each item.

---

## 1. Dashboard Nav Config — FOUND
**Path:** `C:\Users\ziyad\Projects\script-shield-platform\src\components\dashboard\nav-config.ts`

Exports a `DashboardNavItem` interface and a `dashboardNavItems` array of 14 nav entries (Overview, Protector, Keys, Monetization, Stats, Teams, Domains, Changelog, Security, Alerts, Logs, Profile, Webhooks, and an admin-only "Admin" entry). Also exports `getVisibleDashboardNavItems(userRole?)` which filters out `adminOnly` items unless `userRole === "ADMIN"`. Icons are sourced from `lucide-react`. Full contents were retrieved and reported in the tool output above.

---

## 2. "library" references — FOUND (2 files)
A case-insensitive search for "library" across all files matched two files:

- **`C:\Users\ziyad\Projects\script-shield-platform\lua\auth.lua`** — contains references to libraries (e.g. "debug library tampered" checks in the `checkDebugHardening` / `checkFunctionIntegrity` functions). This is the runtime Lua client-side auth/shielding module (ScriptShield) — ~750 lines covering HWID collection, spoofing detection, anti-debug/anti-hook/anti-vm/anti-replay guards, key-prompt UI, AES-GCM payload decryption, and a runtime re-validation loop.
- **`C:\Users\ziyad\Projects\script-shield-platform\src\app\dashboard\scripts\page.tsx`** — the scripts dashboard page. Contains a **"UI Library" selector** where users choose between `FireLab UI` (built-in key prompt UI) and `Custom UI` (the user's own UI library, e.g. Rayfield). State is tracked via `const [uiLibrary, setUiLibrary] = useState<"firelab" | "custom">("firelab")`. Full contents retrieved.

There is **no dedicated `library` page or route** (e.g. no `/dashboard/library`); the only "library" concept is the UI-library toggle inside the scripts page and the "debug library" mentions in the Lua file.

---

## 3. obfuscator.ts — FOUND
**Path:** `C:\Users\ziyad\Projects\script-shield-platform\src\lib\obfuscator.ts`

A Node.js crypto-based Lua obfuscator module. Exports:
- `isLoadStringPayload(source)` — detects existing `--FireLab-Loadstring` / `--FireLab-Obfuscated` markers.
- `obfuscateLua(source, options)` — multi-layer obfuscation: XOR with a random key (16/24/32 bytes by strength standard/strong/maximum), byte rotation, a shuffled custom base64 alphabet, SHA-256 checksum, and randomized Lua variable names. Emits a self-decoding `--FireLab-Obfuscated` Lua payload. Honors an extensive `ObfuscateOptions` type (strength, anti-* flags, expected executors/placeIds/universeIds).
- `generateLoadString(config)` — produces a full Lua loadstring wrapper that fetches an AES-GCM-encrypted module from a gateway URL, with keyless/key modes, a GUI key prompt, HWID collection, HMAC signing, key persistence, and a runtime re-check loop.
- `obfuscateLuaToLoadString(source, options)` — backward-compat alias for `obfuscateLua`.

Full contents retrieved.

---

## 4. scripts API route — FOUND
**Path:** `C:\Users\ziyad\Projects\script-shield-platform\src\app\api\scripts\route.ts`

A Next.js route module with two handlers:
- **`GET`** — authenticates the user, applies rate limiting (`scripts:list`, 60/60s), supports `page`/`take`/`search`/`status` query params. Admins see all scripts; non-admins see scripts they own or that belong to teams they're a member of. Returns sanitized scripts (decrypts stored content; conditionally applies `obfuscateLuaToLoadString` when visibility is `OBFUSCATED`; strips `contentEncrypted` and `contentHash`).
- **`POST`** — authenticates, rate-limits (`scripts:create`, 10/60s), validates a `zod` schema (`name`, `description`, semver `version`, `content` ≤5MB, `obfuscate`, `visibility` PUBLIC/PRIVATE/OBFUSCATED, `status`, `category`, `iconUrl`, `channel` STABLE/BETA, `protectionProfileId`, `teamId`). Optionally obfuscates, encrypts the stored content via `encryptObject`, hashes it, creates the DB record, and writes an audit log (`SCRIPT_CREATE`). Returns the sanitized script.

Full contents retrieved.

---

All requested files were read in full via the `read_file` tool (the complete file contents appear in the tool outputs above). No file modifications were made.

## Summary

I located and read all four requested files in the project at `C:\Users\ziyad\Projects\script-shield-platform`. All files were read in full. Below are the findings for each item.

---

## 1. Dashboard Nav Config — FOUND
**Path:** `C:\Users\ziyad\Projects\script-shield-platform\src\components\dashboard\nav-config.ts`

Exports a `DashboardNavItem` interface and a `dashboardNavItems` array of 14 nav entries (Overview, Protector, Keys, Monetization, Stats, Teams, Domains, Changelog, Security, Alerts, Logs, Profile, Webhooks, and an admin-only "Admin" entry). Also exports `getVisibleDashboardNavItems(userRole?)` which filters out `adminOnly` items unless `userRole === "ADMIN"`. Icons are sourced from `lucide-react`. Full contents were retrieved and reported in the tool output above.

---

## 2. "library" references — FOUND (2 files)
A case-insensitive search for "library" across all files matched two files:

- **`C:\Users\ziyad\Projects\script-shield-platform\lua\auth.lua`** — contains references to libraries (e.g. "debug library tampered" checks in the `checkDebugHardening` / `checkFunctionIntegrity` functions). This is the runtime Lua client-side auth/shielding module (ScriptShield) — ~750 lines covering HWID collection, spoofing detection, anti-debug/anti-hook/anti-vm/anti-replay guards, key-prompt UI, AES-GCM payload decryption, and a runtime re-validation loop.
- **`C:\Users\ziyad\Projects\script-shield-platform\src\app\dashboard\scripts\page.tsx`** — the scripts dashboard page. Contains a **"UI Library" selector** where users choose between `FireLab UI` (built-in key prompt UI) and `Custom UI` (the user's own UI library, e.g. Rayfield). State is tracked via `const [uiLibrary, setUiLibrary] = useState<"firelab" | "custom">("firelab")`. Full contents retrieved.

There is **no dedicated `library` page or route** (e.g. no `/dashboard/library`); the only "library" concept is the UI-library toggle inside the scripts page and the "debug library" mentions in the Lua file.

---

## 3. obfuscator.ts — FOUND
**Path:** `C:\Users\ziyad\Projects\script-shield-platform\src\lib\obfuscator.ts`

A Node.js crypto-based Lua obfuscator module. Exports:
- `isLoadStringPayload(source)` — detects existing `--FireLab-Loadstring` / `--FireLab-Obfuscated` markers.
- `obfuscateLua(source, options)` — multi-layer obfuscation: XOR with a random key (16/24/32 bytes by strength standard/strong/maximum), byte rotation, a shuffled custom base64 alphabet, SHA-256 checksum, and randomized Lua variable names. Emits a self-decoding `--FireLab-Obfuscated` Lua payload. Honors an extensive `ObfuscateOptions` type (strength, anti-* flags, expected executors/placeIds/universeIds).
- `generateLoadString(config)` — produces a full Lua loadstring wrapper that fetches an AES-GCM-encrypted module from a gateway URL, with keyless/key modes, a GUI key prompt, HWID collection, HMAC signing, key persistence, and a runtime re-check loop.
- `obfuscateLuaToLoadString(source, options)` — backward-compat alias for `obfuscateLua`.

Full contents retrieved.

---

## 4. scripts API route — FOUND
**Path:** `C:\Users\ziyad\Projects\script-shield-platform\src\app\api\scripts\route.ts`

A Next.js route module with two handlers:
- **`GET`** — authenticates the user, applies rate limiting (`scripts:list`, 60/60s), supports `page`/`take`/`search`/`status` query params. Admins see all scripts; non-admins see scripts they own or that belong to teams they're a member of. Returns sanitized scripts (decrypts stored content; conditionally applies `obfuscateLuaToLoadString` when visibility is `OBFUSCATED`; strips `contentEncrypted` and `contentHash`).
- **`POST`** — authenticates, rate-limits (`scripts:create`, 10/60s), validates a `zod` schema (`name`, `description`, semver `version`, `content` ≤5MB, `obfuscate`, `visibility` PUBLIC/PRIVATE/OBFUSCATED, `status`, `category`, `iconUrl`, `channel` STABLE/BETA, `protectionProfileId`, `teamId`). Optionally obfuscates, encrypts the stored content via `encryptObject`, hashes it, creates the DB record, and writes an audit log (`SCRIPT_CREATE`). Returns the sanitized script.

Full contents retrieved.

---

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror search content tools. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 100). Use to limit output for broad patterns. When the cap is hit the result is paginated; pass 'offset' set to the previous offset+maxResults to continue.

offset.description: Optional: Number of matching file paths to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text files. For images, PDFs, videos, audio or other multimedia files, this tool cannot present their contents to the model — use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool instead.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once. Note: search_file_content reports matches as 1-based `Lxx` line numbers; to jump from a hit at `L42` here, subtract 1 — pass `offset: 41` (i.e. `offset = lineNumber - 1`).

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact command to execute using the best available Windows shell (automatically selected from PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt). Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. The search backend is ripgrep, so regex patterns use ripgrep/Rust regex syntax, not JavaScript RegExp syntax. If you need to search literal text that contains regex metacharacters (for example $, {, [, (, or \), set fixed_strings: true or -F: true instead of escaping by hand. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. By default, returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers; set output_mode to "files_with_matches" for just file paths or "count" for per-file match totals. Each matched line is truncated at 500 characters with a "[line truncated from N chars]" suffix to keep minified or base64 content from blowing up context.

pattern.description: The regular expression pattern to search for within file contents. This is interpreted by ripgrep/Rust regex, not JavaScript RegExp; avoid JS-only features such as lookbehind and backreferences. Regex syntax errors are returned by ripgrep. For literal text, especially strings containing regex metacharacters like $, {, [, (, or \, prefer fixed_strings: true or -F: true. When passing JSON args, escape only for JSON once: to match literal ${, use pattern "\\$\\{" (the regex engine sees \$\{), not "\\\\$\\\\{".

path.description: Optional: A path relative to the workspace root, or an absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

glob.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Single string only — combine multiple patterns via whitespace/comma separation (e.g. '*.js *.ts', '*.js,*.ts') or brace expansion ('*.{ts,tsx}'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

output_mode.description: Optional: Output format. 'content' (default) returns matching lines with line numbers and optional context; 'files_with_matches' returns only the list of matching file paths; 'count' returns per-file match counts.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 250, max: 1000). Values above 1000 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches. Pass 0 for unlimited.

head_limit.description: Optional: Claude Code aligned alias for 'maxResults'. When both are provided, head_limit wins. Pass 0 for unlimited (use sparingly — large result sets waste context).

offset.description: Optional: Number of matches to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

-A.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -A.

-B.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -B.

-C.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -C.

context.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for -C.

-n.description: Optional: Whether to include line numbers in 'content' output. Defaults to true; set to false to omit them.

-i.description: Optional: Case-insensitive matching. Defaults to false (case-sensitive), matching ripgrep behavior.

fixed_strings.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for -F.

-F.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for fixed_strings.

type.description: Optional: ripgrep file type filter (rg --type). Common types: js, py, rust, go, java, etc. Accepts any alias from `rg --type-list`; the value is forwarded verbatim to ripgrep.

multiline.description: Optional: Enable multiline matching where patterns may span newlines (passes -U and --multiline-dotall to ripgrep). Only meaningful for 'content' output_mode. ^/$ anchors keep line boundaries behavior for portability across search backends.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror ripgrep's default hidden-skip behaviour. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly (passes --hidden to ripgrep).
